figures.caption: Table 1. Quantitative comparisons of different methods on T2I generation quality on the DABench and DiffusionDB datasets. ∗ denotes methods equipped with our modified ESA module.

Figure 1. Overview of our proposed method. Our DiffGraph framework consists of three key components: the Graph Construction Agent (GCA), the Expert Selection Agent (ESA), and the Merging Planner (MP). In the Universal Graph Construction stage, for ease of understanding, we illustrate a simplified example in which GCA collects and organizes N = 8 online experts (indexed as 1–8), and evaluates them on Nr = 3 reference prompts for node calibration. During the Dynamic Subgraph Activation stage, ESA parses user requirements and selects a subset of experts, for example {3, 4, 5, 7}, to participate in the merging process. MP then activates the corresponding subgraph and generates the merging coefficients, which are used to produce the final merged model for image generation.
